title: Handle Duplicate User Email Across Salesforce Orgs
story: As a Salesforce consultant or user with access to multiple Salesforce orgs, I need DataTools Pro to correctly associate my login with the right org, so that I don't accidentally access another org's data.
description:
A user email can exist in multiple Salesforce orgs (common with consultants or sandboxes without suffixed usernames). The dtp_user email field must be unique for the security model.

Enhancement: On first authentication, check the current Salesforce OrgId for the email against dtp_users to see if the email already exists for a different OrgId. Prevent users from authenticating against one Salesforce instance and being logged into DataTools for another org.
acceptance_criteria: On authentication, check if email already exists for a different Salesforce OrgId
Prevent cross-org login where user authenticates against one SF instance but accesses another org's data
Handle the edge case gracefully with a clear message to the user
middleware_changes: Authentication flow checks Salesforce OrgId against existing dtp_users entries for the same email. Blocks cross-org access.
data_model_changes: May require composite uniqueness on email + OrgId rather than email alone on dtp_users.
implementation_notes: This is an edge case primarily affecting consultants with access to multiple Salesforce instances. Not yet implemented.
comment_summary: Product asked developer to look into this. No implementation yet.
